title: australina lanceolata
intro: Australina lanceolata, also known as the native box, is a small tree native to eastern Australia with a compact, rounded crown and a slender, often twisted trunk. The leaves and bark of the Australina lanceolata plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, thanks to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Australina lanceolata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams to leverage its medicinal properties.
